title: Family Audit and Multi-Track Sensitivity Evaluation
summary: This evaluation performs seven sensitivity tracks on the GEV tail-constraint findings from exp_id1. Family Audit finds 1 genuine correction (Afrikaans: Afro-Asiatic→Indo-European), preserving 26 families. Corrected Regression on 172 treebanks confirms word-order entropy as sole significant predictor (beta=0.0835, p_fdr=0.0007, pseudo-R²=0.164), nearly identical to original (beta=0.084, CI overlap confirmed). GoF-Restricted re-fits GEV for 918 combos with KS testing; 259 pass (28.2%), retaining 120 treebanks. Critically, entropy becomes non-significant on GoF-restricted data (p=0.93), indicating the finding depends on poorly-fitting combos. Annotation Sensitivity (131 treebanks, feat_completeness>0.5) shows entropy still significant but attenuated (beta=0.047, p=0.007). LOFO over 26 families: mean beta=0.084, 92.3% significant. Residual Analysis ranks Turkish at position 67 with relative residual 0.44. Spoken/Written excludes en_atis; bootstrap CIs for Slovenian (d=2.74) and French (d=5.01) show consistent direction. Synthesis: 2/4 tracks show entropy significant (corrected, annotation); GoF-restricted and LOFO-all do not, yielding overall_entropy_robust=False. 53 metrics_agg values conform to exp_eval_sol_out schema. Four diagnostic PNGs generated. Completes in 21s on 4 CPUs.
